payload.description: Total YTD variance across all agencies for total overtime

tool_args.label: Non-Reimbursable Overtime Variance Drivers
tool_args.raw_text: NYCT: higher costs due to higher vacancy/absentee coverage requirements and adverse weather events. MTAHQ: lower deployment levels being utilized on overtime for the MTAPD at MTA HQ. MTA Bus: lower costs due to COVID-related cleaning efficiencies, lower maintenance requirements, and lower unscheduled service. MNR: lower expenses due to reduced service overtime needs by train & engine crew, and lower maintenance requirements. B&T: lower costs reflect management efficiencies, improved scheduling and deployment practices, and rescheduled maintenance. LIRR: lower costs due to lower requirements for maintenance, vacancy/absentee coverage, tightened management procedures and a retroactive wage accrual.

payload.claim_text: Non-Reimbursable overtime variance drivers by agency: NYCT higher costs from vacancy/absentee coverage and adverse weather; MTA HQ lower from reduced MTAPD deployment; MTA Bus lower from COVID cleaning efficiencies, lower maintenance, and lower unscheduled service; MNR lower from reduced T&E crew needs and lower maintenance; B&T lower from management efficiencies, improved scheduling, and rescheduled maintenance; LIRR lower from lower maintenance, vacancy coverage, tightened management, and retroactive wage accrual.
payload.description: YTD variance explanations for non-reimbursable overtime by agency

evidence_refs.source_quote: NYCT: higher costs due to higher vacancy/absentee coverage requirements and adverse weather events. MTAHQ: lower deployment levels being utilized on overtime for the MTAPD at MTA HQ. MTA Bus: lower costs due to COVID-related cleaning efficiencies, lower maintenance requirements, and lower unscheduled service. MNR: lower expenses due to reduced service overtime needs by train & engine crew, and lower maintenance requirements. B&T: lower costs reflect management efficiencies, improved scheduling and deployment practices, and rescheduled maintenance. LIRR: lower costs due to lower requirements for maintenance, vacancy/absentee coverage, tightened management procedures and a retroactive wage accrual.

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote must be 280 characters or fewer
evidence_refs[0].source_quote is not present in the cited block

tool_args.label: Total Overtime YTD Variance Drivers
tool_args.raw_text: YTD December costs were $36M below Forecast. Major drivers of the favorable non-reimbursable YTD variance include lower requirements for maintenance, vacancy/absentee coverage, tightened management procedures, and a retroactive wage accrual at the LIRR; managerial efficiencies and rescheduled maintenance at B&T; lower T&E crew requirements at MNR; lower maintenance requirements, and lower unscheduled service at MTA Bus; and, reduced coverage requirements for the MTAPD at MTA HQ, partially offset by higher vacancy/absentee coverage requirements and adverse weather at NYCT. The favorable reimbursable YTD variance results from less capital project activity at MNR and the LIRR, and less MTAPD coverage requirements at MTAHQ, partially offset by higher capital project support requirements at NYCT.

payload.claim_text: Total overtime YTD costs were $36M below Forecast. Favorable non-reimbursable drivers: lower maintenance, vacancy/absentee coverage, tightened management, and retroactive wage accrual at LIRR; managerial efficiencies and rescheduled maintenance at B&T; lower T&E crew at MNR; lower maintenance and unscheduled service at MTA Bus; reduced MTAPD coverage at MTA HQ — partially offset by higher vacancy/absentee coverage and adverse weather at NYCT. Favorable reimbursable variance: less capital project activity at MNR and LIRR, and less MTAPD coverage at MTA HQ, partially offset by higher capital project support at NYCT.
payload.description: Summary of major drivers for total overtime YTD variance

evidence_refs.source_quote: YTD December costs were $36M below Forecast. Major drivers of the favorable non-reimbursable YTD variance include lower requirements for maintenance, vacancy/absentee coverage, tightened management procedures, and a retroactive wage accrual at the LIRR; managerial efficiencies and rescheduled maintenance at B&T; lower T&E crew requirements at MNR; lower maintenance requirements, and lower unscheduled service at MTA Bus; and, reduced coverage requirements for the MTAPD at MTA HQ, partially offset by higher vacancy/absentee coverage requirements and adverse weather at NYCT. The favorable reimbursable YTD variance results from less capital project activity at MNR and the LIRR, and less MTAPD coverage requirements at MTAHQ, partially offset by higher capital project support requirements at NYCT.
